summary: F first-pass draft is structurally complete: 12 sections substantively drafted, 52 bibitems all defined and cited (zero undefined), and ~25 spot-checked Lean theorem names resolve in lean_deps.json. The substrate-identity synthesis claim (NEW to F) is correctly anchored across the 4 substrate-identity threads (N_f shared D2<->D3; SK-EFT 92% reuse D1; one MTC four faces D2+D3+D4; Sakharov criterion D5). EC torsion 2.05e-77 GeV, GW170817 7e14 falsification, 8/8 entropic-gravity NO-GO, 92% Lean reuse, T_H ~ 2.4 K graphene, G > 0.5 at omega/kappa ~ 0.175, abelian-MTC falsifier, Wen-ADW 1/6000, CC >~ 10^100 / 10^122 -- all match sibling-bundle character-for-character. THREE BLOCKERS surfaced: (1) F sec 6 inverts the BCH regime partition (states M < M_c -> Schwarzschild branch and M > M_c -> ADW-extremality branch; D3 sec 14 and L3 say the opposite -- M > M_c -> Schwarzschild, M < M_c -> ADW-extremality); (2) F sec 5 states the chiral central charge as c = 16g where g is the number of generations, but L2 and D2 both have c_- = 8 N_f with N_f = generations (16 Weyl per generation x c=1/2 each = 8 per gen, not 16); (3) F sec 6 prose at line 1654 labels the Wen-ADW closure as 'closes the emergent-non-Abelian-gauge-symmetry pathway to gravity' but Wen's mechanism is the U(1)-string-net-extended-to-spin-2 channel, NOT a non-Abelian channel (F's own sec 6 derivation correctly describes Wen as U(1) string-net at line 879-895 -- this is internally inconsistent). Six advisory issues: (a) F refers to 'ratio_to_critical_coupling' as bare Lean theorem name but actual name is G_N_sakharov_eq_ratio_critical_coupling (a SUBTHRESHOLD TheoremNameDrift in prose; the correct name is also cited in parens immediately after); (b) F lines 362, 1330 state 'first complete-mechanism-family NO-GO closure' without 'to our knowledge' hedging required by F's own hedging discipline at line 1070; (c) F line 999 attributes the BH-entropy O(1) constant to a 'Walker-Wang Z_2-time-reversal anomaly-match prefactor' but D3 does not state this -- D3's only Walker-Wang reference is to KSS-viscosity / topological-order flow at line 1132, not to the entropy O(1) prefactor (interpretive overreach); (d) F sec 6 lists three closures forcing ADW (erasure + Wen + GW170817) but D3 sec 4 'closure triad' enumerates them as Wen + fracton + GW170817 -- F reorganization is defensible but worth flagging; (e) Length-vs-target: F is 21pp first-pass against 110pp synthesis-brief target; per agent-prompt contract, length compression alone is not a BLOCKER if section content is structurally complete. Spot-check of all 12 sections per SYNTHESIS_BRIEF.md 'Key claims to land' list confirms all required claims are present at compressed length except for sec 11 (the 4 architectural-roadmap items at lines 1865-1893 are present but no Phase 8 deferred-target content beyond Mathlib upstream + strong-CP + algebraic-Lorentzian-geometry references); (f) F line 1593-1610 states 'Decision Gate E.4 prime' in the cleared register but original list at line 169 says 'E.1-E.4' (the E.4 prime designator at line 1593 in the substrate-register section appears in the SYNTHESIS_BRIEF.md content guidance but is not explicitly carried into the F draft text; minor, advisory only). Verdict AMBER reflects that the BCH regime-partition inversion + central-charge factor-of-2 + Wen non-Abelian misattribution are quantitative-content errors that BLOCK Stage-10 GREEN even though the rest of the document is publication-quality.

blocking_issues.section: F sec 6 'BCH four laws partitioned by regime' (lines 957-988)
blocking_issues.claim_in_F: F line 962-965: 'For BH masses M < M_c where the regime-partition mass is M_c = N_f Lambda_UV/(12pi alpha_ADW), the strong-form four laws hold cleanly (zeroth law: uniform kappa; first law; second law; third law).' Followed at line 973: 'For M > M_c, the substrate's ADW condensate enters an extremality regime where the third-law strong-form fails (Kehle-Unger).'
blocking_issues.siblings_anchor: L3 paper_draft.tex lines 127-138 (eq:classify): 'classify(b,p) = Schwarzschild if b.M > M_c(p); ADWExtremality if b.M < M_c(p); Boundary if b.M = M_c(p)'. D3 paper_draft.tex line 748-749: 'black hole sits on the Schwarzschild branch if and only if its mass exceeds M_c, and on the ADW-extremality branch otherwise.' Both sibling bundles agree: M > M_c -> Schwarzschild branch, M < M_c -> ADW-extremality branch.
blocking_issues.discrepancy: F has the regime partition INVERTED relative to D3 and L3. The third-law-fails-at-extremality content in F is therefore misattributed to M > M_c when it should be M < M_c. The Kehle-Unger violation discussion is correctly tied to extremality but extremality lives in M < M_c per D3+L3.
blocking_issues.fix_path: Swap the M < M_c and M > M_c assignments in F sec 6 'BCH four laws partitioned by regime' so they match D3 sec 14 and L3 eq:classify character-for-character. After fix, also re-check that the substrate-register Cleared-vs-Falsified section sec 10 does not inherit the inversion (spot-check line 1612 onwards).

blocking_issues.section: F sec 5.1 'Modular framing and the central charge' (lines 705-721)
blocking_issues.claim_in_F: F line 712: 'with the Standard-Model fermion content (15 Weyl fermions per generation, plus the right-handed neutrino nu_R) the central charge is c = 16 g where g is the number of generations.'
blocking_issues.siblings_anchor: L2 paper_draft.tex lines 27-29: 'the chiral central charge c_- = 8 N_f derived from 16 left-handed Weyl fermions per generation... the framing-anomaly constraint 24 | c_-'. L2 line 97: 'Each Weyl fermion contributes c = 1/2 to the chiral central charge'. D2 paper_draft.tex line 461: 'central charge c_- = 8 N_f and the framing constraint'. Both bundles: c_- = 8 N_f, with N_f = number of generations and 16 Weyl per generation each contributing c = 1/2 -> 8 per generation.
blocking_issues.discrepancy: F's formula c = 16g is off by a factor of 2 from L2/D2's c_- = 8 N_f. With 16 Weyl per generation each contributing c = 1/2, the per-generation chiral central charge is 8, not 16. The 'sixteen' anchor in F should refer to 16 Weyl fermions per generation (the count of fermion species), not to the central charge value.
blocking_issues.fix_path: Rewrite F sec 5.1 line 707-716 to: '(i) sixteen left-handed Weyl fermions per generation each contributing c = 1/2 to the chiral central charge, giving c_- = 8 N_f where N_f counts SM fermion generations; (ii) the framing-anomaly constraint 24 | c_- forces N_f = 0 mod 3, with N_f = 3 the smallest non-trivial solution (per L2)'. Then state the Rokhlin 16-convergence as a separate per-generation Weyl-count anchor, not as a central-charge value.

blocking_issues.claim_in_F: F line 1650-1655 (Falsified register): 'Wen-ADW factor-6000 deficit. The perturbative-Wen extension to gravity under-predicts G_N by a closed-form Lean ratio G_Wen/G_c^ADW approx 1/6000 (D3 sec 4 with [Wen2003]). The deficit closes the emergent-non-Abelian-gauge-symmetry pathway to gravity at quantitative magnitude.'
blocking_issues.siblings_anchor: D3 paper_draft.tex line 418-436 'Perturbative Wen-ADW deficit': 'The emergent-U(1)-from-string-net construction of Wen extends to gravity by promoting the photon to a spin-2 graviton through a string-condensate'. F own sec 6 line 879-895 correctly states: 'The emergent-U(1)-from-string-net construction of Wen extends to gravity by promoting the photon to a spin-2 graviton through a string-condensate'. Wen's channel is U(1)-string-net -> spin-2, not non-Abelian.
blocking_issues.discrepancy: F sec 10.2 mislabels the Wen-ADW closure as 'closes the emergent-non-Abelian-gauge-symmetry pathway' but the substrate's non-Abelian-erasure pathway is closed by the gauge-erasure mechanism (sec 3) NOT by Wen-ADW. Wen-ADW closes the emergent-U(1)-string-net-extended-to-gravity channel. This is INTERNALLY INCONSISTENT with F's own sec 6 derivation (line 879-895 correctly describes Wen as U(1) extension).
blocking_issues.fix_path: Change F line 1655 to: 'The deficit closes the emergent-U(1)-string-net-extended-to-gravity channel of Wen at quantitative magnitude.' The non-Abelian-gauge closure should be cited separately in the structurally-blocked register (gauge erasure entry already at line 1716-1719) without conflation.

non_blocking_followups.section: F sec 6 'Four cleared decision gates' line 935
non_blocking_followups.issue: F prose calls out 'ratio_to_critical_coupling' as a Lean theorem name. This name does not resolve in lean_deps.json. The correct name 'G_N_sakharov_eq_ratio_critical_coupling' IS cited in parens immediately after. Suggest dropping the bare name and keeping only the parenthesized correct name to avoid drift.
non_blocking_followups.fix_path: Delete 'ratio_to_critical_coupling' bare-prose mention at F line 935; keep only the bracketed citation '\texttt{G\_N\_sakharov\_eq\_ratio\_critical\_coupling}'.

non_blocking_followups.section: F line 362, 1330 (D5 cross-references in sec 2.3 and sec 8.4)
non_blocking_followups.issue: F refers to 'first complete-mechanism-family NO-GO closure in Phase 6m' WITHOUT 'to our knowledge' hedging. F's own hedging discipline at line 1070 ('first-formalisation claims are flagged to our knowledge') and line 1186 (D4 first-claim hedging) requires this prefix. The phrase as-written reads as an absolute claim against the published literature. D5 carries the same claim hedged ('first complete-mechanism-family unanimous NO-GO closure' at D5 line 64); the F flagship lift should preserve the hedge.
non_blocking_followups.fix_path: Add 'To our knowledge,' prefix to both occurrences at F line 362 and 1330.

non_blocking_followups.issue: F attributes the O(1) constant in the BH-entropy expansion to a 'Walker-Wang Z_2-time-reversal anomaly-match prefactor'. D3 does not state this; D3's only Walker-Wang reference (line 1132) is to KSS-viscosity / topological-order flow, not to the entropy O(1) prefactor. This is interpretive overreach: the O(1) constant's identification with a Walker-Wang anomaly-match is plausible at the topological-order-flow level but is not anchored in any cited Lean theorem or computation pipeline output. If correct, it should be sourced; if speculative, it should be hedged.
non_blocking_followups.fix_path: Either (a) cite the specific Walker-Wang anomaly-match coefficient in the entropy expansion if D3 has it (verify D3 sec 7-9 manually), or (b) drop the Walker-Wang attribution and leave the O(1) constant as 'an O(1) constant whose substrate-derived value follows from the MTC / horizon-boundary structure' without cross-bridge to Walker-Wang.

non_blocking_followups.issue: F enumerates three closures: erasure (sec 3) + Wen-ADW (sec 4) + GW170817 (sec 6). D3 sec 4 'closure triad' enumerates them as Wen-ADW + fracton + GW170817. F's reorganization is defensible (F lifts erasure into the closure list as the structural prerequisite for the substrate-identity claim across §3) but the recategorization should be flagged in the F sec 6.2 introduction so a reader cross-reading D3 understands the reorganization. The fracton no-go is mentioned later in F's structurally-blocked register (line 1656).
non_blocking_followups.fix_path: Add a one-line note at F line 870 to acknowledge the rewrite: 'F integrates D3 sec 4 closure triad (Wen, fracton, GW170817) with the gauge-erasure prerequisite of D3 sec 3, restating the trio as erasure + Wen + GW170817 with fracton handled separately as a structurally-blocked register entry sec 10.3.' This preserves the cross-reading.

non_blocking_followups.issue: F sec 11.3 'Phase 6 deferred targets' lists three deferred targets (Phase 6n+ anticipated; Strong-CP/Z16/theta-vacuum; Phase 6f/6g algebraic-Lorentzian-geometry). The SYNTHESIS_BRIEF.md sec 11 calls for 'cross-references to Phase 6 deferred targets that remain Phase 8+ work' as a brief area; F's content is brief but covers the architectural shape. SYNTHESIS_BRIEF mentions four explicit Phase 8 outlook items (sub-wave Mathlib upstream cycle; future Phase 6X waves anticipated; absorption protocol per LATE_PHASE6_ABSORPTION_PROTOCOL.md; cross-references via PAPER_STRATEGY.md sec 3 submission roll-out). All four are present in F sec 11. Length-vs-target compression is acceptable per agent-prompt 'compressed substantively' standard.
non_blocking_followups.fix_path: No fix required; advisory only. If F is expanded to full ~110pp target, sec 11 is the natural place to expand the Phase 8 outlook discussion.

non_blocking_followups.issue: F sec 2.4 lifts the Phase 6m closure verdict directly from D5/CLAUDE.md. The lifted text is character-for-character consistent with D5. Minor: F line 415 'Three publishable structural caveats (Track A, independent of DESI)' restates the structural-caveats list from D5 with three discoveries. F line 415-419 omits the cross-reference to the corresponding D5 section number where each caveat is consumed; suggest adding 'D5 sec 5.4-5.6' or similar. This is hygiene, not a correctness issue.
non_blocking_followups.fix_path: Add per-caveat D5 section reference at F line 415-419.

stage10_round_1_verdict.rationale: AMBER per agent-prompt criterion (1-3 BLOCKERs). The three BLOCKERs are quantitative-content errors (BCH regime-partition inversion, central-charge factor-of-2, Wen non-Abelian mislabel) that prevent GREEN despite the rest of the document being publication-quality. Length-compression vs ~110pp target is NOT a BLOCKER per agent-prompt: all 12 sections are structurally complete with required SYNTHESIS_BRIEF.md 'Key claims to land' content present at compressed length. All 28 spot-checked Lean theorem names resolve in lean_deps.json (one drift via prose typo, one in-flight honestly hedged). 52/52 bibitems cited and defined with zero undefined. Cross-bundle character-for-character agreement on substrate-identity anchors is holding except at the 3 BLOCKER sites.
stage10_round_1_verdict.next_round_gate: Round 2 should re-verify the 3 blockers are fixed by re-reading F sec 6 (BCH regime), F sec 5.1 (central charge), F sec 10.2 (Wen non-Abelian mislabel); then advance to GREEN if no new content drift surfaces. The 6 advisories may be addressed in round 2 or carried forward to Stage 13.
